plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet mandate. Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure. Add to QQQI, favoring income for capital preservation. Buy IWM for diversification into broad index. Trim QQQ to 15% to manage overall broad index exposure.
conservative.read: This mode targets ~1.5%/month, focusing on capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, but still faces market risk. Expect lower volatility and smaller drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and reduce gross leverage. Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit leveraged ETFs and reduce risk. Trim QQQI due to its downtrend status. Add to CRWD, as it is an uptrending AI/Software name. Buy IWM to add new broad index exposure and diversify.
This plan aims to reduce overall leverage and concentration while maintaining growth potential.
balanced.read: This mode targets ~2.5%/month, balancing risk and growth. It reduces leverage and concentration, but still carries market and sector-specific risks. Expect moderate volatility and drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and reduce gross leverage. Trim QQQI and QQQ due to their downtrend status. Add to CRWD, pressing into an uptrending AI/Software name. Buy IWM and MU, adding high-scoring scanner setups. This strategy leans into strong setups while managing mandated limits.
aggressive.read: This mode targets ~4%/month, pursuing aggressive growth. It maintains higher concentration in strong names and uses leverage. Expect significantly higher volatility, potential for larger drawdowns, and daily decay/rebalancing effects on leveraged ETFs if added.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and enhance stability. Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure. Sell SMH and CRWD to exit high-beta growth names. Add significantly to QQQI, prioritizing income for yield. This plan focuses on generating steady distributions.
income.read: This mode targets ~1%/month, prioritizing steady distributions. It significantly reduces growth exposure and leverage for lower drawdown. Note that distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in market downturns.
